content: src/warren-config/ module mirrors src/projects/ + src/registry/ file layout: errors.ts (WarrenConfigUnavailableError + per-file error type), config.ts (static layout constants — no env vars, .warren/ path is hardcoded relative to project clone), schema.ts (zod schemas + parseTriggersConfig/parseDefaultsConfig returning discriminated ParseResult), load.ts (loadWarrenConfig with injected exists/readFile), index.ts (public re-exports).

title: loadWarrenConfig uses missing-vs-malformed envelope, never throws on per-file errors
rationale: Acceptance criteria #2 of pl-5d74 requires the loader to return null entries for missing files (no error) and validation-error entries for malformed files (no throw). Modeled after src/registry/refresh.ts's per-agent skip-don't-throw pattern: a single malformed sibling never hides a healthy one from the operator. Returned shape: { triggers: T|null, defaults: D|null, errors: WarrenConfigFileError[] }. The HTTP, doctor, and UI layers all render the errors envelope verbatim. Only unrecoverable failures (project clone vanished entirely) throw WarrenConfigUnavailableError — same class shape as ProjectUnavailableError so the HTTP layer can map both to a 503 with the same recovery hint.

content: TriggerSchema is a discriminated union on 'kind' — only 'cron' is implemented today, but the discriminator + .strict() shape is locked in now (per pl-5d74 risk #1) so future webhook/manual triggers added by R-06+ slot in as another 'kind:' without a breaking schema rev. .strict() on every object schema makes typos (e.g. 'defaultRoll') fail loudly instead of silently dropping the field.

Cron expression validation is intentionally loose (5 or 6 whitespace-separated tokens, non-empty) — full grammar is R-06 scheduler's job. Duplicating croner's grammar at the schema layer would lock the upstream version and force a schema rev every time croner ships a syntax extension.

name: lazy-cache-invalidate-before-mutate
description: src/warren-config/cache.ts is a per-project lazy cache around loadWarrenConfig with single-flight semantics. Keyed by project id. invalidate() drops both resolved entries and in-flight promises so a mid-flight load whose envelope is now stale never commits to the cache (identity-comparison on the loadingEntry slot). refreshProject (and spawnRun's pre-refresh) invalidate BEFORE recordRefresh / refreshProjectClone runs so no caller can observe the post-refresh row paired with the pre-refresh parse (pl-5d74 risk #4). deleteProject invalidates after the row delete. Wired through ServerDeps.warrenConfigs; bootServer creates one fresh cache per server. Loader rejections do NOT cache — drop in-flight slot so retries hit the loader.

content: Warren-config lifecycle hooks live on src/projects/manage.ts (refreshProject, deleteProject) and src/runs/spawn.ts (forwards into refreshProject) — all accept an optional warrenConfigs?: WarrenConfigCache and invalidate the project id. The cache itself is server-only (ServerDeps.warrenConfigs); CLI one-shots and tests can omit.

title: ROADMAP sketch said defaults.yaml; shipped as defaults.json
rationale: Format-symmetry decision recorded as part of pl-5d74 wrap-up. defaults is small and structurally flat; matches the rest of os-eco's JSON wire surface; YAML's only advantage (multi-line strings) is moot for the three optional fields shipped today (defaultRole, defaultBranch, defaultPrompt). triggers stays YAML — cron expressions read better there. Documented in ROADMAP R-02, SPEC §11.H, and CHANGELOG [Unreleased].

title: R-02 ships parsed-but-unconsumed defaults + triggers; consumption deferred to R-04 / R-06
rationale: pl-5d74 risk #3 called out scope creep on defaults.defaultRole. Shipped scope: schema, loader, HTTP, UI panel, doctor check, acceptance. Deferred: defaultRole on spawn (still falls back to WARREN_DEFAULT_AGENT), defaultPrompt template substitution, trigger dispatch (R-06's job). This keeps R-02 a foundation seed rather than entangling it with spawn-flow changes that warrant their own seeds. Follow-up seeds filed as part of warren-8107 closure.

content: When R-02 (.warren/) ships new schema fields or new file kinds, update three places in lockstep: ROADMAP R-02 (status + shipped shape + deferred scope list), SPEC §11.H (V1 frozen record — layout/schema/loader/surface/diagnostics blocks), and CHANGELOG [Unreleased] (feat(warren-config) / feat(server) / feat(ui) / feat(diagnostics) entries). The src/warren-config/ file layout (errors/config/schema/load/cache/index) plus the loader's missing-vs-malformed envelope (mx-66d478) are the load-bearing invariants.

defaultRole consumers (R-02): the NewRun UI auto-fills its agent picker (warren-fd14) when the project's .warren/defaults.json declares a defaultRole that matches a registered agent. CLI 'warren run' and scheduled-run dispatch are still deferred to R-04 / R-06 — keep the schema doc in src/warren-config/schema.ts and the R-02 'Scope deliberately deferred' bullet in ROADMAP.md in sync with each new consumer.

DefaultsConfigSchema (src/warren-config/schema.ts) carries defaultProvider + defaultModel alongside defaultRole/defaultBranch/defaultPrompt (warren-618b). Both are non-empty free-text strings; spawnRun (src/runs/spawn.ts readProjectDefaults + resolveOverride) folds them onto agent frontmatter at run-time with precedence operator override > .warren/defaults.json > agent frontmatter, riding the same withProviderOverrides path as per-run overrides. CLI run path wires a fresh WarrenConfigCache by default so 'warren run' honors the same defaults the HTTP server does.

DefaultsConfigSchema (src/warren-config/schema.ts, warren-9993) carries runBranchPrefix alongside defaultRole/defaultBranch/defaultPrompt/defaultProvider/defaultModel. Validation uses RunBranchPrefixSchema (same kebab/snake-case regex as RoleNameSchema — no slashes, since the composed branch shape is `${prefix}/${run.id}` and slashes inside the prefix would muddy the segment count). Empty/whitespace at the env or project level downgrades to the next precedence slot rather than aborting; spawnRun never fails on a bad runBranchPrefix.

WARREN_WORKER_PROBE_* env vars (src/server/probe.ts loadWorkerProbeConfigFromEnv) configure the probe loop: WARREN_WORKER_PROBE_INTERVAL_MS (default 30000), WARREN_WORKER_PROBE_TIMEOUT_MS (default 2000), WARREN_WORKER_PROBE_DISABLED ('1'/'true'). Same Number.parseInt + String(n)===raw guard used by other warren env loaders so '10abc' fails fast.

src/server-config/ (warren-3909) is warren's SERVER-level TOML config loader — distinct from src/warren-config/ (per-PROJECT .warren/ dir loader) and src/server/config.ts (server env vars). Lives next to a warren deployment as warren.toml; opt-in via WARREN_CONFIG_FILE env (no default, preserves zero-config back-compat acceptance #1 of pl-9ba1). Mirrors the src/warren-config/ directory pattern: config.ts (path resolution), errors.ts (re-exports ValidationError), schema.ts (Zod), load.ts (loader), index.ts (re-exports).

title: Server-level TOML loader fails loudly on malformed/missing/schema-reject; per-project loader collects per-file errors
rationale: src/server-config/load.ts (warren-3909) throws ValidationError on any failure (file missing when WARREN_CONFIG_FILE is set, malformed TOML, schema rejection). Contrast with src/warren-config/load.ts which collects per-file errors into an envelope so one bad project doesn't take down the server. Rationale: server-level config is boot-critical — the operator MUST notice and fix it before warren serves requests; supervisor catches the ValidationError, prints it, exits non-zero, docker/fly restart policy retries.

content: src/server-config/schema.ts (warren-3909) ships .strict() and EMPTY in step 7; pl-9ba1 step 8 (warren-272c) adds the [workers] block. The strict-empty stance forces any top-level key (including a premature [workers] usage) to fail loudly with a field-level ValidationError until the schema is extended — risk #6 mitigation (loader lands in isolation with its own test coverage so [workers] step builds on a tested foundation).

src/server-config/workers.ts (warren-272c) splits [[workers]] validation in two layers: schema.ts WorkerEntrySchema.strict() checks shape (name/url non-empty strings), workers.ts validateWorkerEntries enforces cross-row + URL-format checks (name regex /^[a-zA-Z0-9][a-zA-Z0-9_-]*$/, uniqueness, parseWorkerUrl). load.ts runs both after Zod safeParse and exposes ParsedWorkerEntry[] { name, url, transport } on LoadedWarrenServerConfig so the pool factory consumes the parsed Transport without re-parsing.

title: WARREN_BURROW_TOKEN is the operative env var for the [workers] token requirement; the operator-facing name is BURROW_API_TOKEN
rationale: The supervisor (src/supervisor/tokens.ts) requires BURROW_API_TOKEN == WARREN_BURROW_TOKEN and surfaces both names in operator hints. Warren-server reads WARREN_BURROW_TOKEN as the client-side secret it sends to workers. requireSharedBurrowToken (src/server-config/workers.ts) gates on WARREN_BURROW_TOKEN being non-empty when [workers] is non-empty (acceptance #8); the SHARED_BURROW_TOKEN_HINT recovery message names both vars so operators see the supervisor's contract too.

description: parseWorkerUrl initially accepted https:// and rejected port 70000 via a 1..65535 bound; URL constructor throws TypeError on >65535 ports (test caught it) and treats port 443 as the HTTPS default so url.port returns empty string (test for https://host:443 hit the missing-port branch).
resolution: Restrict scheme to unix:// or http://; document defensive port-0 bound (URL spec allows port 0 but TCP doesn't). TLS deferred to V2 when a real driver lands.

content: SPEC §5.4 'Multi-worker model' (warren-dd64) is the architecture-overview section for the worker pool. The §3.2 'No remote burrow workers in V1' non-goal was removed when this section was added. Cross-links burrow-62ce / pl-cb3e for the burrow-side counterpart (TCP bind safety, /admin/drain, OpenAPI updates) and ROADMAP R-12 / §11.J #1 for the design context. If schema fields land on workers/burrows tables, [[workers]] block, or placement rules, update §5.4 in lockstep with the implementation — same convention as mx-5339d5 for §11.H.

PreviewConfigSchema (src/warren-config/schema.ts, warren-7be9 / SPEC §11.L) is a discriminated union on `type` carrying both 'server' and 'static' from day one — V1's launcher only implements server, but the parser accepts static so the static-mode follow-up under pl-2c59 lands without a breaking config rev. Server has command + port (required), readiness_path + idle_ttl + max_lifetime (optional). idle_ttl and max_lifetime stay separate fields by design-lock — do NOT collapse to a single `ttl`. Static is .passthrough() because its shape isn't locked. preview lives under DefaultsConfig.preview (optional); missing block is not an error.

title: DurationStringSchema validates shape only; consumer parses to ms
rationale: warren-7be9 / SPEC §11.L: idle_ttl and max_lifetime are string durations ('30m', '8h', '1h30m'). The schema validates regex shape so malformed input surfaces in the per-file errors envelope (mx-66d478) at load time, but the actual conversion to milliseconds lives with the launcher/eviction worker (warren-ea6b / warren-f156). Compound forms accepted so operators don't pre-do the math. Privileged ports (1-1023) accepted on PreviewPortSchema because the sandbox runs unprivileged-by-namespace.

content: WARREN_PREVIEW_IDLE_TTL / WARREN_PREVIEW_MAX_LIFETIME / WARREN_PREVIEW_MAX_LIVE / WARREN_PREVIEW_EVICTION_TICK_MS / WARREN_PREVIEW_EVICTION_DISABLED (loadPreviewEvictionConfigFromEnv, warren-ea6b) configure the eviction worker. Durations parsed via parseDurationMs; positive-int parser rejects 0/-1; malformed values fail loudly at boot. Per-project preview.idle_ttl/preview.max_lifetime in .warren/defaults.json override the global env at tick time (operator → project default flow).

WARREN_PREVIEW_HOST (src/preview/launch.ts loadPreviewLaunchConfigFromEnv) doubles as the proxy preamble gate: bootServer (src/server/main.ts) only constructs createPreviewProxyHandler + createPreviewAuth when both WARREN_PREVIEW_HOST and WARREN_API_TOKEN are set. --no-auth disables the preview surface (no token to derive the HMAC key from); a warning logs at boot. The login route returns 400 (validation_error) when deps.previewAuth/previewHost are undefined.

loadPrTemplate (src/warren-config/load.ts, warren-bd49) parses .warren/pr-template.md into a Partial<Record<PrFragmentName, string>>. Missing file → prTemplate: null; warnings (unknown_fragment, no_fragments, unclosed_preview_markers) surface in the per-file errors envelope under code: schemaError so they ride the existing checkWarrenConfig doctor path. WARREN_CONFIG_FILES.prTemplate = 'pr-template.md' alongside triggers.yaml + defaults.json.

title: warren-5840: .warren/ YAML reorg with backcompat from defaults.json
rationale: Hoist global defaults into .warren/config.yaml + preview block into .warren/preview.yaml; legacy .warren/defaults.json keeps loading but emits a non-fatal warning. Precedence: config.yaml > defaults.json for defaults; preview.yaml > defaults.preview (from whichever source). YAML beats JSON because the file grows fast (preview, MCP, PR-template …) and JSON's no-comments limitation makes review painful. One file per concern matches .canopy/.mulch/.seeds.

content: LoadedWarrenConfig carries separate errors[] and warnings[] arrays (warren-5840). errors[] are fatal (parse / schema) and flip the warren_config doctor / readyz check to ok=false; warnings[] are advisory (today only the defaults.json deprecation, code warren_config_deprecated) and feed a separate warren_config_deprecations check that stays ok=true regardless. Both arrays use the same { file, code, message } shape so HTTP and UI consumers can render them uniformly.

Loader precedence for .warren/ defaults (warren-5840): src/warren-config/load.ts loadDefaults checks .warren/config.yaml first, falls back to .warren/defaults.json, and appends a warning whenever defaults.json exists at all (whether or not config.yaml shadows it). loadPreviewFile reads .warren/preview.yaml at top level (no nested 'preview:' key) and mergePreviewOverride folds it into defaults.preview, taking precedence over any nested 'preview:' field in config.yaml / defaults.json. preview.yaml without config.yaml synthesizes a defaults envelope with only the preview key set so downstream consumers keep reading defaults?.preview.

title: SPEC §11.L path-mode preview routing (warren-1cce, pl-f4ea)
rationale: Subdomain-mode previews (run-<id>.<host>) require operators to own a domain + wildcard CNAME + DNS-01 wildcard cert — a closed door for zero-domain self-hosters. Path mode (https://<warren-host>/p/<run-id>/...) reuses the single hostname+cert already serving the UI. WARREN_PREVIEW_MODE=path|subdomain (default path) selects between them; subdomain mode stays as the multi-tenant opt-in. Cookie scope narrows from Domain=.<host> to Path=/p/<run-id>/ in path mode; PR annotation, doctor checks, and HTML <base>/Location: rewrites all branch on mode. Eviction, port allocator, and reap sub-steps stay mode-agnostic.

content: PreviewModeSchema (src/warren-config/schema.ts) is z.enum(['path','subdomain']); DEFAULT_PREVIEW_MODE is 'path' (warren-fcb7 / SPEC §11.L path-mode addendum). Accepted as an optional 'mode' field on both ServerPreviewConfigSchema and StaticPreviewConfigSchema discriminated-union members so a project can pin a routing mode in .warren/preview.yaml. Env wins on conflict; merge precedence is enforced at consumption time, not in the schema.

WARREN_PREVIEW_MODE env var (src/preview/launch.ts loadPreviewLaunchConfigFromEnv, warren-fcb7) reads case-insensitive trimmed value; invalid / empty / missing values silently fall back to DEFAULT_PREVIEW_MODE ('path') so a typo never blocks server boot, matching how other WARREN_PREVIEW_* values degrade (mx-d3b88f). PreviewLaunchConfig now carries { host, mode } — host=null is no longer a kill-switch for the preview surface in path mode (the proxy preamble derives origin from request Host).

PreviewReadinessTimeoutSchema (src/warren-config/schema.ts, warren-0928) layers a 1s..1h bounds check on top of DurationStringSchema by calling parseDurationMs (src/preview/duration.ts) in a .refine() — the underlying regex is shape-only. ServerPreviewConfigSchema's readiness_timeout field is the per-project override of DEFAULT_READINESS_TIMEOUT_MS (300_000ms / 5m as of warren-0928, was 60s); reap.ts parses it with parseDurationMs again at the call site since the schema validated bounds at load time.

PreviewSetupTimeoutSchema and PreviewReadinessTimeoutSchema (src/warren-config/schema.ts, warren-d9e7 / warren-0928) use a tryParseDurationMs() helper that swallows parseDurationMs's ValidationError and returns null. Zod runs .refine() even after a sibling .regex() check fails, so the refine must tolerate inputs the regex would have rejected — without the try/catch a malformed duration like 'five minutes' bubbles parseDurationMs's throw all the way up instead of becoming a zod issue.
